metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Address the conflict between AC item 4 ('no hardcoded current-version literal') and tests/unit/test_version_sync.py, which asserts the 'currently <X.Y.Z>' prose marker MUST exist; the plan must either update that test or adopt a replacement strategy that keeps the test green.
findings.finding: The plan's AC 'docs/api-stability.md contains no hardcoded current-version literal' directly conflicts with an existing CI unit test (tests/unit/test_version_sync.py) that requires the 'currently <X.Y.Z>' prose marker to be present and asserts failure if it is absent.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, item 4 / What section 'docs/api-stability.md: remove/replace the stale hardcoded version'
findings.suggested_fix: Option A: Replace the literal with a dynamic include/reference and update test_version_sync.py to match the new lookup strategy. Option B: Change the AC to 'update the literal to match pyproject.toml' (already true) and instead harden the existing test as the long-term maintenance mechanism — then the plan reduces to confirming the test already passes.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `tests/unit/test_version_sync.py` as an edit target: either update it to accept the new version-agnostic wording (or PyPI pointer), or remove it, so the plan's AC ('no hardcoded current-version literal') and CI do not conflict.
findings.finding: tests/unit/test_version_sync.py is an unenumerated consumer of the 'currently X.Y.Z' marker in docs/api-stability.md. The test asserts (hard-fails, not warns) if that exact prose pattern is absent: 'assert m, "could not find a currently <X.Y.Z> version marker in api-stability.md"'. The plan's AC says to remove the hardcoded version literal, but the test requires a parseable version marker to exist, causing a CI break under any 'remove wording' approach, and also under a 'pointer to PyPI' replacement.
findings.location: What / files section: 'docs/api-stability.md: remove/replace the stale hardcoded version' + AC: 'docs/api-stability.md contains no hardcoded current-version literal'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `tests/unit/test_version_sync.py` to the 'What / files' section and decide: (a) if replacing with a dynamic pointer (e.g. `see pyproject.toml`), delete or rewrite the test to check the doc contains that pointer; (b) if keeping a version marker but deferring to pyproject.toml, the test already works correctly and no change is needed — but then the AC should read 'version literal kept in sync via test' not 'no hardcoded literal'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the AC for docs/api-stability.md with tests/unit/test_version_sync.py: the plan must specify whether to (a) keep the `currently <X.Y.Z>` literal and simply update it to match pyproject.toml (preserving the test), (b) delete or rewrite test_version_sync.py alongside removing the literal, or (c) replace the literal with a dynamic reference that the test can still validate — the current AC ('no hardcoded current-version literal') conflicts with the test's hard assert that the prose marker must exist.
findings.finding: The plan's AC for item 3 ('docs/api-stability.md contains no hardcoded current-version literal') directly conflicts with the existing unit test gate in tests/unit/test_version_sync.py, which asserts at line 33 that the 'currently <X.Y.Z>' prose marker MUST exist. Removing the literal without also updating or deleting that test will cause the test to assert-fail in CI, breaking the build.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — item 4 ('docs/api-stability.md contains no hardcoded current-version literal'); tests/unit/test_version_sync.py lines 23–37
findings.suggested_fix: Either update the AC to 'docs/api-stability.md version literal matches pyproject.toml (enforced by test_version_sync.py)' — meaning the fix is simply updating 0.7.1 to the current version — or, if the intent is truly to eliminate the per-release maintenance burden, extend the What/files section to also update test_version_sync.py to use a dynamic pyproject.toml lookup without requiring a prose marker, and update the AC accordingly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify the actual current content of docs/api-stability.md before executing: it already reads 'currently 0.7.1' (not 0.6.0); reconcile whether the goal is (a) fix a stale value (already done) or (b) remove the prose literal entirely — these are different tasks.
findings.finding: The plan states docs/api-stability.md 'hardcodes currently 0.6.0' (a stale version), but the file already reads 'currently 0.7.1', which matches pyproject.toml v0.7.1. The premise is false; no version-text fix is actually needed for staleness.
findings.location: Why section, item 3 / What section / AC item 4
findings.suggested_fix: Update the Why/What to reflect the true state: the version is not stale. If the real intent is to make the doc version-agnostic (remove the literal), say so explicitly as the goal — do not rely on a false staleness premise.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the `-r` flag to the `git rm --cached` command so it reads `git rm -r --cached .joe-janitor/` (or list individual files explicitly) to avoid a fatal 'not removing directory recursively without -r' error.
findings.finding: The plan's `git rm --cached .joe-janitor/` command will fail at runtime: `git rm --cached` refuses to remove a directory recursively without the `-r` flag, producing `fatal: not removing '.joe-janitor/' recursively without -r`. The plan omits this required flag.
findings.location: What / files — first bullet: `git rm --cached .joe-janitor/ tracked files`
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `git rm --cached .joe-janitor/` with `git rm -r --cached .joe-janitor/` in the What / files section and any accompanying runbook steps.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the plan's 'Why' item 3 to reflect that the current file already says 0.7.1 (not 0.6.0), so the rationale accurately describes the actual state motivating the change.
findings.finding: The plan's 'Why' section describes the stale version as '0.6.0' ('hardcodes "currently 0.6.0" while the package is 0.7.1'), but the actual file already reads 'currently 0.7.1'. This is a minor inaccuracy in the plan's stated justification but does not affect the edit target itself.
findings.location: Why section, item 3: 'docs/api-stability.md hardcodes "currently 0.6.0" while the package is 0.7.1'
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'hardcodes "currently 0.6.0" while the package is 0.7.1' to 'hardcodes the version literal "currently 0.7.1" which will drift again without per-release maintenance'.
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend AC4 to also verify the replacement: add a second condition that the doc contains a maintenance-free version reference (e.g. 'see PyPI' or a dynamic pointer) so both removal AND replacement are grep-verifiable.
findings.finding: AC4 verifies only removal of the hardcoded version string but not the replacement: the Why section explicitly calls for 'wording that does not need per-release maintenance (or a pointer to PyPI)', yet the AC is 'contains no hardcoded current-version literal (grep-verifiable)' — an implementer who simply deletes the sentence passes the criterion while violating the described intent.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4 vs Why section item 3 and What/files item 3
findings.suggested_fix: AC4 (revised): 'docs/api-stability.md contains no hardcoded current-version literal AND contains a pointer to the canonical version source (e.g. PyPI badge URL or "see PyPI" text) — both grep-verifiable.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before running `git rm --cached`, add a step to `grep -r '.joe-janitor' --include='*.md' --include='*.txt' .` (excluding .joe-janitor/ itself) and resolve any references found in committed docs.
findings.finding: The plan removes .joe-janitor/ tracked files from the git index but does not verify that no other committed file (README, CONTRIBUTING, CLAUDE.md, etc.) references those paths — if any such reference exists, it becomes a stale link after git rm --cached.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a pre-step: `grep -rn 'joe-janitor' --include='*.md' . | grep -v '^.joe-janitor/'` — if any hits appear in committed docs, update or remove those references as part of this ticket.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State which replacement approach is preferred for the stale version in docs/api-stability.md (e.g., reword as 'see the current release on PyPI at <url>' vs. delete the sentence vs. add a PyPI badge link), so the executor does not have to choose.
findings.finding: The replacement text for the stale version in docs/api-stability.md is not decided — the plan offers two options ('wording that does not need per-release maintenance' OR 'a pointer to PyPI') without choosing one, leaving a real design decision to the executor.
findings.location: What / files section, third bullet: 'remove/replace the stale hardcoded version'; Why section item 3: 'Replace the literal with wording that does not need per-release maintenance (or a pointer to PyPI)'
findings.suggested_fix: In the What/files bullet, replace 'Remove/replace the stale hardcoded version' with a specific instruction such as: 'In docs/api-stability.md, replace the sentence containing the hardcoded version with: "See the current release version on PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/<package-name>/"'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for AC4 that anchors on a version-literal pattern, e.g.: `grep -E '[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' docs/api-stability.md` should return no matches (or document the exact acceptable matches if some version references are intentional).
findings.finding: AC4 claims 'grep-verifiable' but provides no grep command or anchor pattern, leaving the verification open to false-positives (too loose) or false-negatives (misses variant formats like '0.7.x' or bold/code-span markup).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, fourth bullet: 'docs/api-stability.md contains no hardcoded current-version literal (grep-verifiable)'
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC4: 'Verified by: `grep -cE "[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+" docs/api-stability.md` exits 0 only when the count is 0; if any version literals are intentionally retained (e.g. a minimum-version compatibility statement), list them as known exceptions in this AC.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit back-out note to the What/files section: e.g. 'To revert: git add .joe-janitor/ && git commit; remove the .git/info/exclude entry' so the step is reversible by documented procedure.
findings.finding: The `git rm --cached .joe-janitor/` step is a destructive index operation on publicly tracked files but the plan provides no explicit rollback or back-out path, as required by T4 when an irreversible step is called out.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' subsection under What/files: 'To undo: run `git add .joe-janitor/ && git commit -m "restore joe-janitor tracking"` and remove the .joe-janitor/ line from .git/info/exclude.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
